title: Cloud & DevOps for Scalable Frontend-Backend Systems
summary: Gain mastery over VPS deployment, Docker, and CI/CD pipelines to maintain robust, scalable fullstack apps at Truffaire.
personalizationNote: Deepens Sudarshan’s hands-on VPS deployment, Docker, Git and backend experience with cloud-native best practices for frontend lead roles.
videoStyle: Animated deployment pipeline with real server dashboard visuals
learningObjectives: Deploy React and FastAPI applications securely on VPS
Containerize fullstack apps with Docker for consistency
Automate deployments using Git workflows and CI/CD
Monitor system health and scale services on cloud infrastructure
knowledgeChecks: Describe essential VPS security configurations for deployment.
What advantages does Docker provide for your frontend-backend stack?
How can CI/CD with Git improve release reliability?
narrationScript:
Deploying Fullstack Applications on a VPS Securely
Hosting your React frontend and FastAPI backend on the same VPS is efficient, but security comes first. Nginx acts as a reverse proxy managing HTTPS traffic smoothly. Firewalls and SSH keys guard your server against intrusions. These steps lock down your deployment while keeping critical Truffaire features available and fast for users worldwide.

Dockerizing Frontend and Backend for Consistency and Scalability
Docker takes your Development-to-Production journey from painful to smooth. Building concise Dockerfiles for React and FastAPI packages up your apps with their dependencies. Docker Compose spins up the entire stack—databases, caches, backends—in one command. Smaller, optimized images mean quicker startup and easier scaling across Truffaire servers. It’s infrastructure peace of mind made simple.

Setting Up CI/CD Pipelines with Git for Reliable Releases
Imagine every code push running tests and, if they pass, deploying your app without you lifting a finger. GitHub Actions and GitLab CI do exactly that. Automations keep your VPS updated with stable code, while instant alerts notify you on failures. This workflow gives you confidence, speeds delivery, and lets you focus on building innovative features for Truffaire.

slides.title: Deploying Fullstack Applications on a VPS Securely
bullets: Configure Nginx as reverse proxy with HTTPS
Setup firewall and SSH security best practices
Deploy React static files and backend APIs on same VPS
slides.speakerNotes: You’ve deployed apps on VPS before, but here’s how to lock down security and ensure your fullstack apps run fast and reliably for Truffaire’s users.

slides.title: Dockerizing Frontend and Backend for Consistency and Scalability
bullets: Create Dockerfiles for React and FastAPI services
Use Docker Compose to orchestrate multi-container apps including Redis and PostgreSQL
Optimize images for fast builds and minimal size
slides.speakerNotes: Your experience with Docker is strong. This walkthrough shows how to containerize all parts of your stack cohesively for easy scaling and team collaboration at Truffaire.

Docker Compose spins up the entire stack—databases, caches, backends—in one command. Smaller, optimized images mean quicker startup and easier scaling across Truffaire servers. It’s infrastructure peace of mind made simple.

slides.title: Setting Up CI/CD Pipelines with Git for Reliable Releases
bullets: Integrate GitHub Actions or GitLab CI for automated tests
Deploy successful builds automatically to VPS or cloud
Set up notification alerts on failures to react quickly
slides.speakerNotes: You already use Git extensively. CI/CD automation is the next step to ship features faster and catch bugs before they reach Truffaire’s users.
slides.narration: Imagine every code push running tests and, if they pass, deploying your app without you lifting a finger. GitHub Actions and GitLab CI do exactly that. Automations keep your VPS updated with stable code, while instant alerts notify you on failures. This workflow gives you confidence, speeds delivery, and lets you focus on building innovative features for Truffaire.
